title: alopecurus alpinus subsp. pseudobrachystachyus
intro: Alopecurus alpinus subsp. pseudobrachystachyus, also known as alpine foxtail or false brachystachyus foxtail, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Alopecurus alpinus subsp. pseudobrachystachyus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially alleviating symptoms of various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a topical cream to harness its medicinal properties.
